message.content:
# Mobile Responsiveness Analysis: Wordle Analyzer

## Overall Assessment
The Wordle Analyzer mobile view appears well-optimized with no major layout issues. The design effectively adapts to the 375x812 viewport, maintaining readability and functionality.

## Specific Checks:

### Text Overflow
- No text overflow issues detected
- All text content fits within the viewport boundaries
- The title "Wordle Analyzer" displays properly with appropriate line breaks

### Layout & Spacing
- Adequate spacing between elements
- The grid layout (Wordle game board) is properly sized and centered
- No cramped elements or overlapping content

### Touch Targets
- The hamburger menu icon (☰) in the top-right appears appropriately sized for touch interaction
- The "Analyze" button has sufficient tap area (green button)
- The "Hard Mode" checkbox has adequate spacing around it
- The "Clear" button at the bottom is properly sized

### Navigation
- Hamburger menu is present and correctly positioned in the top-right corner
- Menu icon is clearly visible and accessible

### Grid/Cell Sizing
- The 5x6 Wordle grid cells are properly sized and spaced
- Cells maintain consistent dimensions across the grid
- No distortion or misalignment of grid elements

### Horizontal Scroll Issues
- No horizontal scrolling required
- Content fits perfectly within the mobile viewport width
- No overflow causing side-to-side scrolling

### Visual Problems
- Consistent color scheme (green accent color matches branding)
- Clear visual hierarchy with proper font sizing
- Good contrast between text and background
- The "Last updated" and "Trusted by" information is properly formatted

## Recommendations
- The current implementation shows good mobile responsiveness
- No critical issues requiring immediate attention
- The layout effectively utilizes the available screen real estate
- Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile interaction

The mobile view successfully maintains the core functionality and visual design of the desktop version while adapting optimally for smaller screens.
